You are able to update your Wix Payments account settings (personal details, business details, bank account information, business description) at any time. 
  
  To update your bank details or any of the mentioned settings:
  1. Go to [Accept Payments] (https://users.wix.com/signin?originUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.wix.com%2Fmy-account%2Fsite-selector%3FbuttonText%3DSet+Up+Payments%26title%3DSelect+a+Site%26autoSelectOnSingleSite%3Dtrue%26actionUrl%3Dhttps%3A%2F%2Fwww.wix.com%2Fdashboard%2F%7B%7BmetaSiteId%7D%7D%2Fpayments%2F&postLogin=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.wix.com%2Fmy-account%2Fsite-selector%3FbuttonText%3DSet%2BUp%2BPayments%26title%3DSelect%2Ba%2BSite%26autoSelectOnSingleSite%3Dtrue%26actionUrl%3Dhttps%253A%252F%252Fwww.wix.com%252Fdashboard%252F%257B%257BmetaSiteId%257D%257D%252Fpayments%252F&overrideLocale=en&forceRender=true) in your site's dashboard.
  2. Click Manage next to Wix Payments.
  3. Click Edit Account Details.
  4. Edit the relevant information and click Save.
  Tip: Use our [guidelines for uploading documents] (https://support.wix.com/en/article/guidelines-for-uploading-documentation-for-wix-payments) to ensure your files are uploaded successfully.
  5. Click Resubmit.
  
  Please make sure the bank account holder’s name matches the owner of the Wix Payments account.
  
  Bare in mind that whenever you make a change, Wix is required to verify your information. Because of this, your account status temporarily goes back to Accepting Payments. During this time, you can accept payments through your site, but you cannot receive payouts to your bank account. The verification process can take up to 7 business days. Once it is complete, you will be able to receive any payouts accumulated during this time.

contexts.text:
Wix Payments: Updating Your Account Settings
For regulatory or security reasons, Wix Payments may periodically require information to keep your account verified. You may also wish to update your account settings if there has been a change in your circumstances, such as a change of address or bank account.You can go back and update any of your Wix Payments account settings (personal details, business details, bank account information, business description) at any time. To update your account settings:
Go to Accept Payments in your site's dashboard. Click Manage next to Wix Payments.
 Click Edit Account Details. Edit the relevant information and click Save.
Tip: Use our guidelines for uploading documents to ensure your files are uploaded successfully. Click Resubmit.Important:Whenever you make a change, Wix is required to verify your information. Because of this, your account status temporarily goes back to Accepting Payments. During this time, you can accept payments through your site, but you cannot receive payouts to your bank account. The verification process can take up to 7 business days. Once it is complete, you will be able to receive any payouts accumulated during this time.
contexts.title: Wix Payments: Updating Your Account Settings

contexts.text:
Wix Payments: Guidelines for Uploading Documentation
Wix may request that you send the following documents in order to verify your Wix Payments account. The photo ID is used to verify that the identity of the Wix Payments account owner (personal or business) matches the identity of the bank account owner.Important:All documents* must be issued within the same country you select as your business location. Documents may be rejected if issued from another country.(*For a business Wix Payments account, your ID may be issued from any Wix Payments supported country).Learn about the guidelines for uploading documents for:
Photo identification Address verification  Bank details Company detailsPhoto IDYour photo ID must meet the following requirements:
Your photo ID can be a passport, driver's license or a government issued ID card Your photo ID must be active and non-expired The photo must be in color Your ID must show all 4 borders and corners The ID must not have information blocked or blacked out The ID must be straightened, not angled or rotated The ID must not be blurry, overexposed or copied from another source (e.g. screenshot) Your first, middle and last name in your Wix Payments account must match those of your ID Your birth date in your Wix Payments account must match that of your ID For passports with a Machine-Readable Zone (MRZ), the MRZ must be visible. For driver's licenses or government issue ID cards, send a separate file for each side Documents must be sent in one of the following: HEIC, JPEG, JPG, PDF, PNG The maximum file size is 10 MB The minimum file size is 1 KB for PDF, 100 KB for other formatsAddress verification documentProvide a document, such as utility bill, which verifies the address you have submitted. Your address verification document should meet the following requirements:
Documents must be visible and clear Documents must be dated within the last six months Complete documents must be uploaded. A complete document is defined as:
    
One full page of the document (at minimum) The full name and address of the individual are clear and visible   Documents must be sent in one of the following: HEIC, JPEG, JPG, PDF, PNGUsers in Canada must provide separate documents for identity and address verificationBank detailsTo verify your bank details, you can provide one of the following documents:
A bank statement A voided check Official letters
contexts.title: Wix Payments: Guidelines for Uploading Documentation

answer: According to the Google Ads with Wix refund policy, payments made for Google Ads campaigns are non-refundable. If you have remaining ad credits, you can pause your campaign and resume it later using those credits. For more details, you can refer to the [Google Ads with Wix refund policy](https://support.wix.com/en/article/google-ads-with-wix-refund-policy#google-ads-with-wix-refund-policy).

contexts.text:
Google Ads with Wix: Refund Policy
Learn more about the Google Ads with Wix refund policy as well as what happens to any remaining ad credit if you pause a campaign.Learn more about:
Google Ads with Wix refund policy Frequently asked questions (FAQs)Google Ads with Wix refund policyAd credits added to Google Ads with Wix are paid with a monthly subscription or a one-time payment. These payments are non-refundable.If you pause a campaign, you have the option at any time to resume the campaign using only your remaining Google ad credits.Additional Information:
Wix gets 15% of your payment for running and optimizing your campaign, the remaining 85% goes towards your ad budget which is spent on ad clicks. Prices do not include VAT, which is determined based on your billing country. To prevent your campaign being disapproved by Google, make sure the campaign complies with Google's Advertising Policies.FAQsClick a question to learn more about Google Ads with Wix policy, budget changes and more.How do I view my Google Ads with Wix invoices?All invoices for Wix services can be viewed in the Billing History page of your account. Invoices may include VAT, which is determined based on your billing country.What happens to my ad credit if I pause a campaign?You can pause your campaign at any time. As soon as you pause, the following changes happen:
Your ad is no longer shown on Google. Your remaining ad credits remain in your account. If the paused campaign was your last active campaign on the account, auto renew is turned off for the subscription.Note: Any Google Ads credits that have an expiry date may expire while the campaign is paused.What is the difference between my monthly subscription and a one-time payment?Your monthly subscription is the amount billed monthly for all the campaigns you're running with Google Ads with Wix. The monthly subscription amount is applied directly to the total ad credit each month (ad credit available across all the campaigns).
However, if you want to purchase additional ad credit, you can make a one-time purchase of credits that are added to the total in your account. You can then readjust the daily budget of one or more campaigns as needed. This won’t affect your overall monthly subscription.
contexts.title: Google Ads with Wix: Refund Policy

question: I am not satisfied with the product and would like to request a refund for my Google Ads campaign.

answer:
To cancel your Premium subscription within the 14-day period, you should follow these steps:

1. Go to [Premium Subscriptions](https://premium.wix.com/wix/api/mpContainerStaticController?originWidgetName=billings) in your Wix account.
2. Click the More Actions icon next to the relevant plan.
3. Click **Cancel Plan**.
4. Click **Cancel Now** to confirm.

If you cancel within the first 14 days, your plan will be canceled immediately, and you will receive a full refund within the next 20 business days. If you are unable to find the cancel feature, ensure you are logged into the correct account and have the necessary permissions. If the issue persists, consider reaching out to Wix support for further assistance. [Learn more about the 14-day refund policy](https://support.wix.com/en/article/requesting-a-refund-for-a-premium-plan).

contexts.text:
Canceling a Wix Premium or Studio Plan
You can cancel your Premium plan or Studio plan at any time. Both plans come with a 14-day money back period guarantee. If you cancel after 14 days, the plan remains active until the end of the billing cycle (yearly or monthly). The plan remains active until the expiration date.After canceling, you can still change your mind any time before the expiration date by turning the auto-renewal back on. See information on payment dates in your Billing History page. You can view the expiration date from your Premium Subscriptions page.

        Click to cancel your plan now

Unassigning a plan does not cancel the plan.Will I lose my site content if I cancel my plan?No, canceling your plan does not delete your site content. Your site will revert to the free Wix URL and display Wix ads, but all your content will remain intact.Before you cancel, you may want to try:Are you sure you want to cancel? You may be interested in other options for optimizing your site, instead of canceling your plan.Switching to a monthly billing cycleSwitch from a yearly to a monthly billing cycle to make payments more manageable. While our longer-term plans offer great savings, with a monthly plan you don't have to commit to a long-term subscription. answer:
To enable customers to purchase multiple quantities of the same product at a discounted rate, such as 'Buy 3 hats, get 20% off', you can use the automatic discounts feature in Wix Stores. Here’s how you can set it up:

1. Go to your site's dashboard and navigate to [Automatic Discounts](https://www.wix.com/my-account/site-selector/?buttonText=Manage%20Discounts&title=Select%20a%20Site&autoSelectOnSingleSite=true&actionUrl=https://www.wix.com/dashboard/{{metaSiteId}}/discounts).
2. Click **Create Discount**.
3. Select **Standard discount** and click **Continue**.
4. Enter a discount name that customers will see during checkout.
5. Select **Specific products** and choose the product (e.g., hats) that the discount applies to.
6. Check the **Minimum quantity of items** checkbox and enter '3' as the minimum quantity.
7. Select **Percentage off** from the discount type drop-down and enter '20%' as the discount.
8. Set the start and end dates for the discount, or leave it open-ended.
9. Click **Save** to apply the discount.

contexts.text:
Wix Stores: Creating Automatic Discounts
Create automatic discounts so customers can get price reductions without having to enter a coupon code. When customers buy products that qualify for discounts, they automatically appear.Show me how to access automatic discounts
Go to your site's dashboard. Click Marketing. Select Discounts. Click Automatic Discounts.
In this article, learn how to:
Create standard automatic discounts Create "Buy X, Get Y" discounts Manually turn discounts on and off Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)Creating standard automatic discountsCreate an automatic discount that applies to all your products, a category, or specific products you choose. You decide what the discount is and when it can be applied.To create automatic discounts:
Go to Automatic Discounts in your site's dashboard. Click Create Discount. Select Standard discount and click Continue.
Tip: The Discount type is preselected. Enter the discount name.
Tip: Customers see this name during checkout. Select what the discount applies to:
    
All products: The discount applies to each product that a customer buys (i.e. applies to multiple products in the same order). Specific products: Click to select one or more products that the discount can be applied to. Specific categories: Click to select one or more categories that the discount can be applied to.   (Optional) Create minimum requirements for the discount to apply:
    
Minimum quantity (e.g. buy 3 hats get $10 off):

Select the Minimum quantity of items checkbox. Enter the number of items.   Minimum amount (e.g. spend $50 on hats and get $10 off):

Select the Minimum order subtotal checkbox. Enter the minimum amount.     Select the type of discount from the What is the discount? drop-down and enter the required percentage, amount, or price.
Note: If you offer an Amount off discount (e.g. $10 off), it's applied to every relevant item in an order. Select the date and time when the discount starts. (Optional) Select the dates when the discount ends:
Note: By default, the discount starts immediately and doesn't have an end.
    
Select the Set an end date and time checkbox. Select the date and time when the discount ends.   Select where customers see the sale name and details: Click Save.Creating "Buy X Get Y" discountsReward customers for their purchases by offering something for free or at a reduced rate when they make certain

answer:
To reinstall the Wix Blog app to address loading issues, you can follow these steps:

1. **Remove the Existing Blog App:**
   - Go to your Wix Editor.
   - In the Pages menu, locate the Blog page and the Post page.
   - If these pages are not visible, you may need to delete the existing blog setup. You can find instructions on how to delete the Wix Blog [here](https://support.wix.com/en/article/wix-blog-deleting-your-blog).

2. **Reinstall the Blog App:**
   - Go to the Wix App Market from your editor or directly from the Wix App Market website.
   - Search for 'Wix Blog' in the search bar.
   - Select the Wix Blog app from the search results and click 'Add to Site'.
   - Follow the prompts to complete the installation.

3. **Check for Loading Issues:**
   - Ensure your Wix app is updated to the latest version by checking the Apple App Store or Google Play.
   - Make sure your device supports the Wix app (iOS 11 and above for iPhone, Android 6.0 and above for Android).
   - If issues persist, try logging out and back into the Wix app, or clear the cache on your device.

answer:
If you cannot see your paid ad in the campaign list, it might be because it is still under review by Google. When you create a new campaign, it appears in the campaigns page with a status label such as 'In Review' until the campaign content is approved by Google. You can check the status of your campaign on the campaigns page in your site's dashboard. For more details, you can refer to the section on creating multiple campaigns [here](https://support.wix.com/en/article/google-ads-with-wix-creating-multiple-campaigns#creating-multiple-google-ads-with-wix-campaigns).

Regarding payment, your Google Ads with Wix subscription is billed monthly, and the amount is applied directly to your ad credit each month. If you have set up a monthly subscription, the payment will be automatically deducted from your credit card. However, if you need additional ad credits beyond your subscription, you can make a one-time payment to top up your credits. You can manage your subscription and make one-time payments from the campaigns page in your dashboard. For more information, you can visit the section on managing your subscription [here](https://support.wix.com/en/article/google-ads-with-wix-faqs-about-your-monthly-subscription-daily-spend-and-ad-credits#your-monthly-subscription-ad-credit-and-daily-spend).

contexts.text:
Google Ads with Wix: FAQs About Your Monthly Subscription, Daily Spend, and Ad Credits
From the main campaigns page in your Google Ads with Wix dashboard you can view and manage your subscription. You can also adjust your daily spend across all your campaigns and make one-time payments.Click a section below to learn more about your Google Ads with Wix subscription, daily spend, and ad credits.In this article, learn more about:
Your monthly subscription, ad credit, and daily spend Ad credits for live or paused campaigns How your ad spend works Making one-time payments The Google Ads $500 promotional credit Currency questionsYour monthly subscription, ad credit, and daily spendWhat is the difference between a monthly subscription, ad credit, and daily spend?
Monthly subscription: The amount billed monthly (before VAT) as a whole for all campaigns you're running with Google Ads with Wix. The monthly subscription amount is applied directly to your ad credit each month. Ad credit: The total sum of money available to run ads across all your campaigns. Ad credit is only spent on ad link clicks and clicks to CTA buttons. Note: Your ad credit is paid in USD regardless of the currency you pay your subscription in. One-time ad credits: A one-time purchase of extra credits for when you want to promote a specific sale or event, or give a specific campaign(s) a boost and get more clicks.  Daily spend: The amount of money you set for a specific campaign to spend on clicks per day. You can set a different daily spend for each campaign using your ad credit. Your daily spend is optimized to be spent only on ad link clicks and CTA button clicks. The amount will be paid up front, and is not pay-per-click.What does my Google Ads with Wix subscription include?Your Google Ads with Wix subscription includes:
The amount billed monthly (before VAT) as a whole for all campaigns you run with Google Ads with Wix. The amount is applied directly to your ad credit each month.  The Wix fee* and tax.*Wix gets 15% of your payment for running and optimizing your campaign. The remaining 85% goes towards your ad credit which is spent on ad clicks.Does Wix charge a fee?Wix gets 15% of your payment for running and optimizing your campaign, the remaining 85% goes towards your ad spend which is spent on ad clicks. Prices do not include VAT, which is determined based on your billing country.What does

Promote a specific sale or service: Create one Google Ads with Wix campaign to generate more site traffic overall, and use another campaign to promote a specific sale or offering happening that month. Manage one subscription and budget for all campaigns: You don't have to worry about paying multiple subscriptions for each campaign. You pay one subscription and choose your budget. Any live campaigns appear at the top of the list in chronological order.From the campaigns page you can also manage your Google Ads with Wix subscription and budget as well as create a new campaign.Creating multiple Google Ads with Wix campaignsYou can create as many campaigns as you want from Google Ads in your site's dashboard. Keep track of your spend for each campaign as well as the campaign status. For every new campaign, choose a daily budget that is allocated from your total ad credits.To create multiple campaigns:

To change your page URL:
1. [Go to your editor](https://manage.wix.com/account/site-selector/?actionUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.wix.com%2Feditor%2F%7BmetaSiteId%7D%3FeditorSessionId%3D%7Besi%7D%26).

4. Click SEO Basics.
5. Enter the page URL under URL slug (last part of the page's URL).
6. Press Enter on your keyboard.

contexts.text:
Wix Editor: Changing Your Page URL
Each page on your site has its own unique URL. If you change a page's URL, Wix creates an automatic 301 redirect, taking visitors and search engines from the old URL to the new one. This helps prevent visitors and search engines from getting a 404 error if they go to an older broken link. Learn more about search engine optimization (SEO). Important: You must first upgrade your site and connect a domain to your site for the redirects to be created.In this article, learn more about:
Changing your page URL URL paths to avoidChanging your page URLCustomize the URL of your pages from their page settings in your editor.To change your page URL: 
Go to your editor. Click Pages & Menu  on the left side of the editor. Hover over the relevant page and click the More Actions icon . Click SEO Basics. Enter the page URL under URL slug (last part of the page's URL). Press Enter on your keyboard.Tip:Use words related to the page's content to increase the chances of that page showing up in relevant search results. URL paths to avoidWhen you change your site page URL, you should keep URL paths in mind. It's not possible to use certain URL paths for your pages, as they're used by Wix apps or reserved for SEO. Some examples of URL paths you should avoid for your pages are:
apps _api robots.txt sitemap.xml feed.xml sites events (when using the Wix Events app) event-details (when using the Wix Events app)If you try to use these URL paths, you'll see an error that the URL's unavailable.
contexts.title: Wix Editor: Changing Your Page URL

To update the picture that appears when you share your website link, you need to change the og:image settings in your Wix Editor. 
Here are the steps you can follow:

1. **For an individual page:**
   - Go to your Wix Editor and click on 'Pages & Menu' on the left side.
   - Hover over the relevant page and click the More Actions icon.
   - Choose 'Social Share' and click 'Upload Image' to select a new image.
   - Click 'Add to Page'.
   - [Publish](https://support.wix.com/en/article/wix-editor-saving-previewing-and-publishing-your-site#publishing-your-site) your site.

2. **For the whole site:**
   - Open [your site's settings](https://www.wix.com/my-account/site-selector/?buttonText=Edit%20Site%20Info&title=Select%20a%20Site&autoSelectOnSingleSite=true&actionUrl=https://www.wix.com/dashboard/{{metaSiteId}}/settings/) and click on 'Website settings'.
   - Scroll down to 'General social image' and click 'Upload Image'.
   - Select a new image or upload a new one, then click 'Choose File' and 'Save'.
   - Go to your editor and [publish your site](https://support.wix.com/en/article/wix-editor-saving-previewing-and-publishing-your-site#publishing-your-site).

contexts.text:
Customizing the Social Share Settings for Your Site's Pages
When you share your pages to a social network, Wix provides the information you've included in your Social Share settings for the page. You can customize the text and images that display when you share your site's pages on social networks like Facebook, Instagram, and Twitter. In this article, learn more about:
Social Share Settings Customizing your page's Social Share SettingsAbout Social Share SettingsOpen graph (og) tags are used by social networks to display text and images when a page is shared. By default, the values you use in your SEO title tags and meta descriptions are used in your Social Share og:title and og:description.You can customize the following Social Share settings: 
og:image: Choose an image that reflects the topic of the page. Use the preview to see how the image will look in your social share.  og:title: Add a title that summarizes the content of the page. We recommend keeping the length below 80 characters or some of the title may be truncated. Use the preview to see how the title will look in your social share. og:description: Add a short description of the content on the page. We recommend keeping the length below 300 characters or some of the description may be truncated. Use the preview to see how the description will look in your social share.Important:Each social media channel uses its own criteria to choose the best title, description, and image for the pages that get shared on it. While it's likely the information in your Social Share settings will be used, it's not guaranteed.Customizing Your Page's Social Share SettingsGo to the Social Share section in your SEO Settings to customize your page's Social Share settings.To customize a page's Social Share settings:
Go to Search Engine Optimization (SEO) in your site's dashboard. Scroll down to Tools and settings. Click Go to SEO Settings under SEO Settings. Select the page type that you want to edit. 
Note: You will only see options for the pages that are part of your site. For example, if you added Wix Stores to your site, you'll see an option to customize settings for Products. Click Edit next to Basics & social share. Scroll down to Social share and customize the relevant settings: Social shareYou can use static text to apply the same title and description to every page, or use variables to create rules to use differe

To access your site history in Wix, you can do the following:

- From the Wix Editor: Click 'Site' on the top of the Editor, then click 'Site History'.
- From the Studio Editor: Click the Wix Studio logo on the top left, hover over 'Site', and click 'Site History'.
- From your site's dashboard: Go to 'Site & App', scroll down, and click 'Site History'.
Once there, you can browse through the recent revisions to your site, see who made the changes, and when they were made. Click the Arrow icon next to the version you want to view.

contexts.text:
Viewing and Managing Your Site History
Your site history is a record of every time that you (or someone working on your site) has saved or published within your Editor. You can access your site history to check when a change was made, who performed the change or to restore a previous version.

        Click to go to site history
    


        View site history
      

Viewing your site historyYou can access your site history from your Editor or site's dashboard.Wix EditorStudio EditorDashboard
Click Site on the top of the Editor. Click Site History.
Click Go to Site History. 
Click the Wix Studio logo on the top left of the Studio Editor. Click Site. Click Site History.
Go to Site & App in your site's dashboard. Scroll down and click Site History.Once there, you can browse through the recent revisions to your site, see who made the changes and when they were made. Click the Arrow icon  next to the version you want to view.Restoring an older site versionRevert your site back by restoring a previously saved or published version. Keep in mind that restoring a version erases any changes made after that specific version.Important: When you restore your site, some elements are not affected. To restore a site version:
Go to your site history. Click the Arrow icon  next to the relevant version. Review the revision to make sure it's the one you want to restore. Click Restore.Tips:
To filter site history, click the Show drop-down menu. You can filter to find saved, published, and starred versions. To find a specific site version again in the future, click the Rename icon , and give it a memorable name.Viewing site changes by collaboratorsYou can use site history to see which collaborators made changes your site. When a version is saved, it is date and time stamped, and includes the email of person who performed the update.To view site changes made by collaborators:
Go to your site history. View the collaborator's email address on the relevant version.
contexts.title: Viewing and Managing Your Site History

To clear your browser's cache:
Close all windows and tabs of your browser.
Open a new tab, ensuring Wix.com is not open.
Follow the steps for your browser below:

Chrome:
Press Ctrl + Shift + Del (press Cmd + Shift + Delete using a Mac).
Click the drop-down next to Time range and select All time.
Make sure the box is checked next to Cached images and files.
Click Clear data.
A screenshot showing how to clear data in Google Chrome.
Close and reopen Chrome. 

FireFox:
Press Ctrl + Shift + Del (press Cmd + Shift + Delete using a Mac).
Click the drop-down next to Time range to clear: and select Everything. 
Make sure the box is checked next to Cache.
Click Clear now.
A screenshot showing how to clear cache in Firefox.
Close and reopen Firefox. 

Safari:
Press Cmd + , to open Preferences. 
Click the Advanced tab. 
Check the box next to Show Develop menu in menu bar (at the bottom of the window).
Click Develop in Safari's menu bar.
 Click Empty Caches.
A screenshot showing how to clear cache in Safai.
Close and reopen Safari. 

Edge:
Press Ctrl + Shift + Del (press Cmd + Shift + Delete using a Mac).
Make sure the box is checked next to Cached data and files and click Clear. 
Close and reopen Microsoft Edge.

contexts.text:
Clearing Your Browser's Cache
Your browser caches data about each website you visit to speed up loading times. Over time, the actual data online can differ from what your browser has cached, potentially causing local issues. Clear your browser's cache periodically and whenever you experience website issues.Before clearing cache:Open your browser in incognito/private mode to see if the issue is cache related. If the problem is resolved in incognito mode, follow the steps to clear your browser's cache.To clear your browser's cache:
Close all windows and tabs of your browser. Open a new tab, ensuring Wix.com is not open. Follow the steps for your browser below:ChromeFirefoxSafariEdge
Press Ctrl + Shift + Del (press Cmd + Shift + Delete using a Mac). Click the drop-down next to Time range and select All time. Make sure the box is checked next to Cached images and files. Click Clear data.
Close and reopen Chrome. 
Press Ctrl + Shift + Del (press Cmd + Shift + Delete using a Mac). Click the drop-down next to Time range to clear: and select Everything.  Make sure the box is checked next to Cache. Click Clear now.
Close and reopen Firefox. 
Press Cmd + , to open Preferences.  Click the Advanced tab.  Check the box next to Show Develop menu in menu bar (at the bottom of the window). Click Develop in Safari's menu bar.  Click Empty Caches.
Close and reopen Safari. 
Press Ctrl + Shift + Del (press Cmd + Shift + Delete using a Mac). Make sure the box is checked next to Cached data and files and click Clear.  Close and reopen Microsoft Edge. Note:Wix supports viewing live sites on Microsoft Edge, however, we do not support the ADI builder on Microsoft Edge.Still experiencing issues after clearing cache?Try flushing your system's DNS. If this doesn't resolve the issues, follow the instructions for disabling add-ons and extensions in the Wix Technical Assistant. Note:Wix no longer supports live sites on IE11. We recommend using one of our supported browsers to view and edit your site.
contexts.title: Clearing Your Browser's Cache

contexts.text:
Wix Editor: Copying and Pasting Elements and Pages to Another Site
If you're building multiple sites using the Wix Editor that require similar elements, pages or themes, you can copy and paste content between them. This allows you to keep the design you like over several sites, without needing to add and design everything again from scratch.In this article, learn more about:
Copying and pasting an element to another site Copying and pasting a page to another site FAQs (Frequently Asked Questions)Copying and pasting an element to another siteIf you add or customize an element that you like, you can copy and paste it to another site in your Wix account. Paste single elements, or multi-select elements to quickly build content.To copy and paste an element: 
Open the Editor that contains the relevant elements. Click the element you want to copy, or multi-select several elements. Right-click and select Copy, or press Ctrl+C (Cmd+C on a Mac). Open the Editor and page you want to paste the elements to. Paste the elements by doing one of the following:
    
Right-click and select Paste, or press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on a Mac). Right-click and select Paste & Apply Site Theme, or press Ctrl+Shift+V (Cmd+Shift+V on a Mac) to apply the color and text themes on the target site.   When pasting elements that contain media files, choose to either:
    
Don't Upload: Paste without uploading the files to the Media Manager of the target site. Upload: Paste and upload the files to the Media Manager of the target site.  You cannot copy the following elements:
Third-party apps Code in Velo by Wix Headers and footers Wix business solution apps (e.g. Wix Stores, Wix Bookings, Wix Restaurants etc). Datasets and collections Code on elements and pages  Wix Pro GalleryCopying and pasting a page to another siteCopy and paste pages as needed between your sites, as long as both are in the Wix Editor. When pasting a page on the target site, you can choose whether to apply the site's colors and fonts or keep the original design. Note:You can only copy regular pages. You cannot copy:
Anchors, links, folders, lightboxes or router pages Pages from third-party and Wix business solution apps (e.g. Wix Stores, Wix Bookings, Wix Restaurants etc).To copy and paste a page:
Click Menus & Pages  on the left side of the Editor. Right-click the relevant page and click Copy. Open the Editor of the site you want
contexts.title: Wix Editor: Copying and Pasting Elements and Pages to Another Site

Wix Editor: Managing Your Lightboxes

Opening lightboxes in the editor Renaming a lightbox Linking elements to a lightbox Duplicating a lightbox Deleting a lightbox Adding a link to the lightbox Close buttonOpening your lightboxesAccess your lightbox from the Menus & Pages panel to edit its design and content, or manage it as needed.To open a lightbox:

Click the relevant lightbox to open it.Renaming a lightboxChange the name of a lightbox so it's easy to find in your editor. Click Done.Linking elements to a lightboxLink an element on your site to a lightbox so it pops up when visitors click the element. You can link text, buttons, and even Vector art to your lightbox.To link an element to a lightbox:

Click Done.Did you know?You can link from one lightbox to another with the Close button, so visitors see a second pop-up when closing the first one. Set the Close button to open the second lightbox when visitors click it using the Link panel. Duplicating a lightboxDuplicate your lightbox to have a consistent design for your pop-ups. You can change the content as needed, while maintaining your favorite text and colors. To duplicate a lightbox:
Click the relevant lightbox in the Pages & Menu  panel. Click the More Actions icon . Click Duplicate. Enter the name for the new lightbox. Click Done.Deleting a lightboxDelete any lightboxes you no longer need to keep your site's announcements fresh and up to date.To delete a lightbox:
Click the relevant lightbox in the Pag

answer:
If you delete an element by mistake, you can revert the change by [clicking the Undo] (https://support.wix.com/en/article/wix-editor-using-undo-and-redo) icon in the top bar of the Editor. **Note:** You can only undo changes you've made to your site in the current session. For example, if you make a change in the Editor, then close the tab or sign out of your Wix account, it is not possible to undo that change when you open the Editor again.

You can also restore your site version:
Go to your [site history](https://www.wix.com/my-account/site-selector/?buttonText=Manage%20Site%20History&title=Select%20a%20Site&autoSelectOnSingleSite=true&actionUrl=https://www.wix.com/dashboard/{{metaSiteId}}/site-history).
Click the Arrow icon  next to the relevant version.
Review the revision to make sure it's the one you want to restore.
Click Restore.

contexts.text:
Wix Editor: Using Undo and Redo
Easily revert or reinstate changes with the Undo  and Redo  icons in the Wix Editor. These allow you to undo and redo changes you have made on your site - whether it's changing the color of a button, or the content of your text.In this article, learn more about:
Undoing a change Redoing a change Text issues when using undo and redo FAQsUsing the Undo featureUse the Undo feature to revert a change you have made in your Editor. For example, if you change the design of an element, and decide you want to revert one of the design options, you can use the feature to do so, instead of manually changing it back.To undo a change you have made, simply click the Undo icon  at the top of your Editor, or press Ctrl + Z on your keyboard.Using the Redo featureUse the Redo feature to reinstate changes you may have undone. This is helpful if you've undone a change by accident, or if you decide you prefer the change the way it was before. To redo an action, click the Redo icon  at the top of your Editor, or press Ctrl + Y on your keyboard.Issues with text elements when using Undo or RedoYou may notice issues with text elements on your site when you use Undo or Redo. This is rare, but if it happens, please contact us with the following information:
The exact change you are attempting to undo/redo A description of the affected text box/string of text The location of the affected text box on your site A screenshot or video of the issueFAQsHow many times can I use Undo and Redo?There are no limits to the number of times you can undo and redo actions in the Editor. However, you can only perform these actions for changes you have made in the current session. This means that if you make a change in the Editor, then close the tab or sign out of your Wix account, it is not possible to undo that change when you open the Editor again.How can I see all the edits and changes I have made in my Editor?You can find an overview of all edits and changes that you have made to your saved and published site in your site history. This makes it easy for you to view, flag, or restore a prior version of your site. Learn how to access your site history
contexts.title: Wix Editor: Using Undo and Redo

Wix Editor: Adding and Deleting Elements
The Wix Editor contains hundreds of unique, customizable elements that you can use to create your site. Adding elements Deleting elementsAdding an element to your siteChoose from a wealth of beautiful, customizable elements and add them to your site in just a couple of clicks.To add an element:
Go to your editor. Click Add Elements  on the left side of the editor. Click a tab, e.g., Gallery, Interactive, Box etc. Click an element or drag it to your site to add it.Tip:Some element tabs (such as text and buttons) have themed elements. These elements have been handpicked for you, as they match your site's color and text theme.What's next?Once you have added an element to your site, use the options to customize and set it up.Deleting elements from your siteYou can delete an element you add to your site at any time. Delete elements individually, or select multiple and delete them all at once. Click an option below to learn more:Deleting elements individually
Select the element in your editor. Press Delete on your keyboard.Deleting multiple elements at once
Hold down the Ctrl key. Click all the elements you want to delete. Press Delete on your keyboard.Deleted an element by mistake?If you delete an element by mistake, you can easily undo this by clicking the Undo  icon in the top bar of the editor.

Before you begin:
You must have a Facebook account to connect an Instagram business account. 
If you haven't done so already, convert your [Instagram personal account to a business account](https://support.wix.com/en/article/connecting-your-instagram-feed-to-your-instagram-business-account#troubleshooting-instagram-business-account-issues) and [connect it to a Facebook](https://help.instagram.com/570895513091465/) page.
If you have an Instagram creator account, you can follow the steps below to connect it. 

If you haven't yet connected your Instagram Feed to any of your accounts (business or personal), follow the steps below.
Click Add Apps   on the left side of the editor.
Search for the Instagram Feed app in the search bar.
Click Add to Site.
Select the app on the page.
Click Settings.
Click Connect Business Account.
Choose your profile in the Log In With Facebook pop-up.
Select the Instagram business account profile you want to use. 
Important:
You should select only one Instagram account.
Select the Facebook page that is connected to the Instagram account you chose in step 8.  
Important:
You should select only one Facebook page.
Click Next.
Review the permissions that the Instagram Feed app requires. 
Click Done.
Click OK.

Click Next. Review the permissions that the Instagram Feed app requires. 
Click Done. Click OK.

question: I want to update the Wix Editor to the new Wix Studio Editor.

answer:
There are 2 ways you can prevent visitors from seeing a new page:
1. [Hide the page](https://support.wix.com/en/article/wix-editor-hiding-and-unhiding-a-page-from-your-site-menu#hiding-a-page-from-your-site-menu-1) from your site menu.
- To hide a page from your menu:
  * Go to your editor.
  * Click Pages & Menu    on the left side of the editor.
  * Click the More Actions icon  next to the relevant page.
  * Select Hide from menu .
2. [Prevent search engines from indexing](https://support.wix.com/en/article/wix-editor-preventing-search-engines-from-indexing-your-page) the page, so it doesn't show up in search results.
- To prevent search engines from indexing a page:
  * Go to your editor.
  * Click Pages & Menu  on the left side of the editor.
  * Click the More Actions icon  next to the relevant page.
  * Click SEO Basics.
  * Disable the Let search engines index this page toggle. 

This way, no one will be able to find the page unless you give them a direct link.

contexts.text:
Wix Editor: Preventing Search Engines from Indexing Your Page
By default, search engines like Google crawl and index every page on your live Wix site. If you don't want a specific page to appear in search results, you can prevent search engines from indexing it in your Editor. From your site's dashboard, you can also choose to prevent search engines from indexing your entire site or use your site's SEO Settings to prevent search engines from indexing groups of pages. Preventing search engines from indexing your entire site disables the options to prevent indexing groups of pages or individual pages. It also disables the option to prevent search engines from indexing those pages individually.Important:
Password protected pages and private member pages don't show in search results, as they are not crawled and indexed. However, you'll need to manually prevent search engines from indexing public member pages such as Followers and Profile. Pages that search engines are prevented from indexing may not appear in your sitemap.To prevent search engines from indexing a page:
Click Pages  on the left side of the Editor. Click the More Actions icon  next to the relevant page. Click SEO Basics. Disable the Let search engines index this page toggle. Want to let search engines index your page again?Enable the Let search engines index this page toggle to make the page visible to search engines again.
contexts.title: Wix Editor: Preventing Search Engines from Indexing Your Page

contexts.text:
Wix Editor: Hiding and Unhiding a Page From Your Site Menu
Every page that appears in the Site Menu section of the Pages & Menu panel, automatically appears on your site's menu. You can choose to hide a page from your site's menu, for example if you are still working on it, or if you do not need that page on your site right now.In this article, learn more about:
Hiding a page from your menu Unhiding a page from your menuTip:Keep in mind that sites with a lot of pages, regardless of whether they are hidden from the menu or not, may affect your experience in the editor. Read our guidelines for creating a multi-page site.Hiding a page from your site menuHide a page so that it doesn't appear on your site's menu. Your site visitors cannot access hidden pages unless you link to them. Note:Hidden pages still appear in search engines. If you don't want your site to appear in search results, you can prevent search engines from indexing your site. Learn how to hide a page from search enginesTo hide a page from your menu:
Go to your editor. Click Pages & Menu    on the left side of the editor. Click the More Actions icon  next to the relevant page. Select Hide from menu .Note:Hiding a page on the desktop version of your site does not mean that the page will be hidden on the mobile version. Learn how to hide a page on your mobile siteUnhiding a page in your site menuEasily unhide a hidden page whenever you want.To unhide a page from your menu:
Go to your editor. Click Pages & Menu    on the left side of the editor. Click the More Actions icon  next to the relevant hidden page.
Tip: A Hidden icon  indicates hidden pages in this panel. Select Show in menu .
contexts.title: Wix Editor: Hiding and Unhiding a Page From Your Site Menu

contexts.text:
Wix Editor: Managing Your Pages in the Mobile Editor
Pages you add to your desktop site are automatically added to the mobile version of your site. You can manage and edit these pages in the mobile Editor. Hide pages and change their layout to customize the experience for visitors on your mobile site.In this article, learn more about:
Viewing pages in the mobile Editor Adjusting the page layout Hiding your site's pages on mobileImportant:It is not possible to change page settings on the mobile Editor. If you would like to adjust your page's settings, you need to do so from the desktop Editor.Viewing your site's pages on mobileView your mobile site's pages to access and make changes to them. You can switch between pages with the Menus and Pages panel.To view a site page on mobile:
Open the mobile Editor. Click Menus & Pages  on the left side of the mobile Editor. Select a page to view it.Note:You can also use the quick access Switch Page drop-down on the top bar of the mobile Editor. Adjusting your pagesChange your page layout and adjust its height to ensure that your site is well aligned for smaller screen sizes. Select an option to learn more.Adjusting the page layout
Open the mobile Editor. Click Page Layout Optimizer  in the left menu of your mobile Editor. Click Optimize Now. Editing the page height
Open the mobile Editor. Select the page body. Click and drag the Page Height icon  to adjust the page size.Hiding pages on mobileThe desktop and mobile-friendly views are different views of the same site. This means that if you delete a page from your desktop view, it also deletes it from your mobile-friendly view. However, you can hide a page from your mobile menu if you do not want it to be accessible on mobile.To hide a page from your mobile site:
Open the mobile Editor. Click Menus & Pages  on the left side of the mobile Editor. Click the relevant page. Click the More Actions icon . Click Hide on mobile .
contexts.title: Wix Editor: Managing Your Pages in the Mobile Editor

answer:
To set up an email for website messages to go to instead of yours, you can add a new email address as a site collaborator. Follow these steps:

1. [Go to Inbox](https://www.wix.com/my-account/site-selector/?buttonText=Go%20to%20Inbox&title=Select%20a%20Site&autoSelectOnSingleSite=true&actionUrl=https://www.wix.com/dashboard/%7B%7BmetaSiteId%7D%7D/inbox/) in your site's dashboard.
2. Click the **Settings** drop-down at the top right.
3. Click **Notifications**.
4. (Optional) Click the **Email notifications** toggle to disable email notifications if you no longer want to receive email notifications to your main account email.
5. Click the **Profile & team** tab.
6. Click **+ Invite People** next to **Your team**.
7. Enter a business email or an alternative email where you want to receive chat and inbox notifications.
8. Click **Send Invite**.
9. Go to the new email's inbox and click **Accept Now** in the email.
10. Log in to the Wix account that's associated with the new email address and click **Accept**.
11. The new email address is automatically assigned a Back Office Manager role and appears in the **Your team** list. Click the toggle under **Email notifications** to turn on email notifications for this email.

Switching Your Site Template

Learn how to copy and paste elements and pages to a new site.You can also right-click on any element or section and save it to My Designs. They will be saved in a design library that you can use on any Editor site in your account. Copying elements from an Editor X siteIn Editor X, you can save and  re-use design assets in a library that you can use on any of your Editor X sites. This saves you time as you don't need to create elements, groups of elements, or sections from scratch.Note: While you can't copy entire pages from Editor X, you can save entire sections as design assets that you can use on your new site.Copying elements from a Studio Editor siteIn Studio Editor, you can save and re-use design assets in a library that you can use on any of your Studio Editor sites. contexts.text:
Wix Editor: Using Undo and Redo
Easily revert or reinstate changes with the Undo  and Redo  icons in the Wix Editor. These allow you to undo and redo changes you have made on your site - whether it's changing the color of a button, or the content of your text.In this article, learn more about:
Undoing a change Redoing a change Text issues when using undo and redo FAQsUsing the Undo featureUse the Undo feature to revert a change you have made in your Editor. For example, if you change the design of an element, and decide you want to revert one of the design options, you can use the feature to do so, instead of manually changing it back.To undo a change you have made, simply click the Undo icon  at the top of your Editor, or press Ctrl + Z on your keyboard.Using the Redo featureUse the Redo feature to reinstate changes you may have undone. This is helpful if you've undone a change by accident, or if you decide you prefer the change the way it was before. To redo an action, click the Redo icon  at the top of your Editor, or press Ctrl + Y on your keyboard.Issues with text elements when using Undo or RedoYou may notice issues with text elements on your site when you use Undo or Redo. This is rare, but if it happens, please contact us with the following information:
The exact change you are attempting to undo/redo A description of the affected text box/string of text The location of the affected text box on your site A screenshot or video of the issueFAQsHow many times can I use Undo and Redo?There are no limits to the number of times you can undo and redo actions in the Editor. However, you can only perform these actions for changes you have made in the current session. This means that if you make a change in the Editor, then close the tab or sign out of your Wix account, it is not possible to undo that change when you open the Editor again.How can I see all the edits and changes I have made in my Editor?You can find an overview of all edits and changes that you have made to your saved and published site in your site history. This makes it easy for you to view, flag, or restore a prior version of your site. Learn how to access your site history
contexts.title: Wix Editor: Using Undo and Redo

answer:
Wix sites built in the Wix Editor are becoming more and more responsive as we continue to add more new and exciting full-width elements which expand to fill the entire width of the screen.
We recommend using full-width elements on your site. If you are using elements such as text boxes and images that can’t be stretched to full width, make sure to keep them within the vertical grid lines so that your elements appear correctly on most screen sizes. [Click here](https://support.wix.com/en/article/wix-editor-working-with-the-editor-gridlines) to learn more.

The following elements are full-width and responsive:
Strips and columns
Full-width slideshows
Galleries
Horizontal menus and lines
Google Maps and other Wix apps

Notes:
Since non-responsive elements (such as images, text and shapes) do not adjust based on screen width, placing these elements on top of a responsive element, such as a strip, may cause differences in the way your page looks in different browsers or on different devices. 

Check out Wix Studio:
If you are an agency or enterprise, you can build responsive sites using the Studio Editor in Wix Studio. This flexible, end-to-end solution includes smart design capabilities and flexible dev tools that let you deliver exceptional results for clients in any industry. [Check our guided tour](https://support.wix.com/en/article/studio-editor-a-guided-tour) introducing the new Studio Editor.

If you are using elements such as text boxes and images that can’t be stretched to full width, make sure to keep them within the vertical grid lines so that your elements appear correctly on most screen sizes. Click here to learn more.The following elements are full-width and responsive:Strips and columnsAdd strips to your site and split them into columns. You can define how big you want each column and arrange your content accordingly. If you are adding elements to a strip or column, make sure to attach them.Full-width slideshowsSlideshows can be used for almost anything. You may want to use them to spice up your homepage, promote special deals or display presentations. GalleriesThe Wix Pro Gallery and many other galleries are responsive. If the gallery has a Stretch icon  it is responsive and you can make it full-width. Click here to learn more. Horizontal menus and linesHorizontal menus and lines automatically adjust to the screen width. Google Maps and other Wix appsGoogle Maps and other apps created by Wix are responsive. If the app has a Stretch icon  it is responsive and you can make it full-width. Click here to learn more. Notes:Since non-responsive elements (such as images, text and shapes) do not adjust based on screen width, placing these elements on top of a responsive element, such as a strip, may cause differences in the way your page looks in different browsers or on different devices. Check out Wix Studio:If you are an agency or enterprise, you can build responsive sites using the Studio Editor in Wix Studio. This flexible, end-to-end solution includes smart design capabilities and flexible dev tools that let you deliver exceptional results for clients in any industry. Check our guided tour introducing the new Studio Editor.We are always working to update and improve our products and your feedback is greatly appreciated.

contexts.text:
Wix Editor: Copying and Pasting Elements and Pages to Another Site
If you're building multiple sites using the Wix Editor that require similar elements, pages or themes, you can copy and paste content between them. This allows you to keep the design you like over several sites, without needing to add and design everything again from scratch.In this article, learn more about:
Copying and pasting an element to another site Copying and pasting a page to another site FAQs (Frequently Asked Questions)Copying and pasting an element to another siteIf you add or customize an element that you like, you can copy and paste it to another site in your Wix account. Paste single elements, or multi-select elements to quickly build content.To copy and paste an element: 
Open the Editor that contains the relevant elements. Click the element you want to copy, or multi-select several elements. Right-click and select Copy, or press Ctrl+C (Cmd+C on a Mac). Open the Editor and page you want to paste the elements to. Paste the elements by doing one of the following:
    
Right-click and select Paste, or press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on a Mac). Right-click and select Paste & Apply Site Theme, or press Ctrl+Shift+V (Cmd+Shift+V on a Mac) to apply the color and text themes on the target site.   When pasting elements that contain media files, choose to either:
    
Don't Upload: Paste without uploading the files to the Media Manager of the target site. Upload: Paste and upload the files to the Media Manager of the target site.  You cannot copy the following elements:
Third-party apps Code in Velo by Wix Headers and footers Wix business solution apps (e.g. Wix Stores, Wix Bookings, Wix Restaurants etc). Datasets and collections Code on elements and pages  Wix Pro GalleryCopying and pasting a page to another siteCopy and paste pages as needed between your sites, as long as both are in the Wix Editor. When pasting a page on the target site, you can choose whether to apply the site's colors and fonts or keep the original design. Note:You can only copy regular pages. You cannot copy:
Anchors, links, folders, lightboxes or router pages Pages from third-party and Wix business solution apps (e.g. Wix Stores, Wix Bookings, Wix Restaurants etc).To copy and paste a page:
Click Menus & Pages  on the left side of the Editor. Right-click the relevant page and click Copy. Open the Editor of the site you want

Wix Inbox: Connecting Instagram to Inbox
Connect your Instagram page to Wix Inbox to manage Instagram messages in the same place as your other communication channels. This keeps your customer interactions organized and easier to manage, and prevents you from having to switch between platforms.All messages sent to your Instagram page appear in your Wix Inbox. From your inbox, you have the option to respond via Instagram or other channels, such as email or chat.In this article, learn more about:
Connecting your Instagram page to Wix Inbox Managing Instagram messages in Wix Inbox TroubleshootingImportant:
In order to connect Instagram to your inbox, you must have an Instagram professional account that is connected to a Facebook page.  Make sure to use a desktop computer rather than mobile device to connect Wix Inbox to Instagram.Connecting Instagram to Wix InboxTo connect your Instagram page, follow the link from your Wix Inbox settings. You'll be redirected to  log in to your Facebook account and complete the setup.To connect Instagram to Wix Inbox:
Go to Inbox in your site's dashboard. ﻿Click the Settings drop-down at the top right. Click Communication Channels. Click Connect Account next to Instagram Business. Log in to Facebook and follow the steps to connect your account.Managing Instagram messages in Wix InboxView and reply to messages sent to your Instagram page, directly from Wix Inbox. As with every other inbox message, you can star important conversations, assign a conversation to someone on your team, archive irrelevant messages, and more.The person who sent you the message is automatically saved as a new contact on your site. You can always edit their contact info to add their phone number, email, etc.To manage Instagram messages in Wix Inbox:
Go to Inbox in your site's dashboard. Choose what you want to do:View and reply to a conversation
Select the relevant conversation to view it. Use the toolbar at the bottom to reply to the conversation:
    
(Optional) Click Instagram next to Message via: to reply using another channel (e.g. email, SMS).


 Enter your message in the Type your message... field.  (Optional) Attach an item, such as a coupon or product link, to your message. Click Send.  Manage your Instagram messagesManage your Instagram messages in the same way you manage your other messages in Wix Inbox. Save contacts, mark messages as unread,

contexts.text:
Opening Your Browser in Incognito/Private Browsing Mode
Sometimes, when trying to view or work on your site, the browser you're using can cause problems. To check if the problem is related to your browser, you can use incognito or private browsing mode. This lets you view your site without your browser remembering previous activity, helping you identify if the issue is caused by your browser.If the issue doesn't happen in incognito / private browsing mode, then it's likely a local problem that can be fixed with the help of the Wix Technical Assistant. Open your browser in incognito or private browsing mode to check if the issues you're experiencing are related to your browser. ChromeFirefoxSafariEdge
Open Google Chrome. Press Ctrl + Shift + N on your keyboard (Cmd + Shift + N on Mac). 
Open Firefox.  Press Ctrl + Shift + P on your keyboard (Cmd + Shift + P on Mac). 
Open Safari. Press Cmd + Shift + N on your keyboard.
Open Microsoft Edge. Press Ctrl + Shift + N on your keyboard.Note:Wix no longer supports live sites on IE11. We recommend using a supported browser to view and edit your site. Still need help?If you're experiencing issues with your live site in incognito/private mode, contact Wix Customer Care and provide us with the following details:
The exact issue you are experiencing. The different browsers and browser versions you have tried. 
Note: Go to whatismybrowser.com to find out what browser version you are using.  The internet security programs you have installed on your computer (e.g. antivirus, firewall). A screenshot of the issue.
contexts.title: Opening Your Browser in Incognito/Private Browsing Mode

question: I'm experiencing slow performance and unresponsive pages in the Wix Editor. How do I open my browser in incognito mode to troubleshoot this issue?

answer:
If you receive an ""error 404 not found"" message when attempting to go to your live site, it means the page doesn't exist on the server at the URL you've entered. This error message is an HTTP standard response code that most commonly occurs when clicking an outdated link that leads to a URL where a page used to exist.

To resolve a 404 error message, try the following:
1. Reload the page by pressing **Ctrl** + **F5** (or **Cmd** + **R** on a Mac).
2. Check that the URL you are trying to access is correct, then re-enter it in your address bar and press **Enter**.
3. If you're trying to access a page other than your homepage, go to the homepage URL (e.g., www.mystunningwebsite.com), then access the page via a different link.
4. If you received a 404 error on a dynamic page, click [here](https://support.wix.com/en/article/cms-formerly-content-manager-troubleshooting-dynamic-pages-issues#404-or-500-error-page) to learn how to resolve the issue.
5. Use another [supported browser](https://support.wix.com/en/article/supported-browsers). If the error only occurs in one browser, [clear your browser's cache](https://support.wix.com/en/article/clearing-your-browsers-cache).

If the problem persists, [contact us](https://support.wix.com/en/article/contacting-wix-customer-care-for-support) and we will be happy to help.

contexts.text:
Live Site Error: Error 404 (Not Found)
If you receive an "error 404 not found" message when attempting to go to your live site, it means the page doesn't exist on the server at the URL you've entered. This error message is an HTTP standard response code that most commonly occurs when clicking an outdated link that leads to a URL where a page used to exist. Common reasons for receiving a 404 errorReview the most common reasons you may see a 404 error below: 
You haven't published your site yet.  You entered the wrong URL (web address) in your browser's address bar.  Your domain isn't properly connected. Learn how to troubleshoot your connection.  You clicked a link from search engine results that has been cached from a previously connected site. Learn more about old URLs appearing in search results.  Your page no longer exists (it was either deleted or the page URL was changed). If you changed your page URL after updating your site URLs, learn how to resolve the issue. Resolving 404 error messagesTo resolve a 404 error message, try the following:
Reload the page by pressing Ctrl + F5 (or Cmd + R on a Mac). Check that the URL you are trying to access is correct, then re-enter it in your address bar and press Enter. If you're trying to access a page other than your homepage, go to the homepage URL (e.g., www.mystunningwebsite.com), then access the page via a different link. If you received a 404 error on a dynamic page, click here to learn how to resolve the issue.  Use another supported browser. If the error only occurs in one browser, clear your browser's cache. Still need help?If the problem persists, contact us and we will be happy to help.
contexts.title: Live Site Error: Error 404 (Not Found)

If you are experiencing issues with your images on your site, please follow the steps below:
1. Make sure that the files you are uploading meet the requirements. Click [here](https://support.wix.com/en/article/wix-media-supported-media-file-types-and-file-sizes) for guidelines.

Wix Editor: Image Issues
If you are experiencing issues with your images on your site, please follow the steps below:
Make sure that the files you are uploading meet the requirements. Click here for guidelines. Try viewing your site on another device to see if the image issue occurs there as well. Click any "?" inside the editor and type your problem. You might find the answer you are looking for. Follow our general interactive tutorial here.If you are still having problems after completing the steps above, click here to contact Customer Care. Please also provide a screenshot of the issue.

"A 403 error occurs when a visitor does not have the relevant permissions to access a collection that appears on the page. Edit the permissions to allow the visitor to access the page. 

1. [Go to CMS](https://www.wix.com/my-account/site-selector/?buttonText=Go%20to%20CMS&title=Select%20a%20Site&autoSelectOnSingleSite=true&actionUrl=https://www.wix.com/dashboard/%7B%7BmetaSiteId%7D%7D/database/) in your site's dashboard.
2. Click the relevant collection.
3. Click **More Actions** at the top and select **Permissions & privacy**.

Make sure the URL (web address) you entered is correct. Clear your browser's [cache](https://support.wix.com/en/article/clearing-your-browsers-cache) and [cookies](https://support.wix.com/en/article/clearing-wix-cookies-from-your-browser). Go to the web address you are trying to view again. If the problem persists, [contact us](https://support.wix.com/en/article/contacting-wix-customer-care-for-support) and we will be happy to help."

contexts.text:
CMS: Troubleshooting Dynamic Pages Issues
Dynamic pages in the Content Management System (CMS) allow you to display multiple items without having to redesign your page for each one. By creating unique pages based on a single design, you save time and ensure consistency across your site. However, like any tool, you might encounter some issues while using dynamic pages. This article helps you identify and resolve common problems, enabling you to fully leverage the efficiency and consistency that dynamic pages bring to your site management.Tip: You can troubleshoot many dynamic page issues from the new View Connections page in your collection. This feature is not yet available in all Wix accounts.  
Go to CMS in your site's dashboard.  Click the relevant collection. Click More Actions at the top right and select View Connections. Review the dynamic page URLs, the fields used as URL slugs, and more.In this article, learn how to resolve the following issues with dynamic pages: 
Live site isn't working properly Dynamic list page navigates to the wrong dynamic item page Dynamic list page links go to the same item Dynamic page dataset incorrectly shows "100 pages generated" Button links don't work or lead to the wrong location Empty dynamic item page Non-functional navigational buttons 403 forbidden error message 404 or 500 error page Large space / gap at the bottom of the pageLive site isn't working properlyIf your page and content is displayed properly in Preview mode but not on your live site, make sure:
Your site has been published since you added the dynamic page.  Check that your collection permissions are set to allow the relevant people to view the content. If you have the optional sandbox collection enabled, sync your sandbox collection to the live collection.Dynamic list page links to the wrong dynamic item pageMisconfigured links on your dynamic list page can cause the wrong dynamic item page URL to load. Make sure your dynamic list page links connect to the dynamic page dataset, and the relevant item page. Also make sure each item has a unique URL. Show me how to fix
Make sure the linked buttons or images on your dynamic list page's repeater, gallery, or table are connected to the dynamic page dataset. Learn how to connect a repeater on your dynamic page to the dynamic page dataset.  Make sure the dynamic page links are connected in the dynamic dataset t
contexts.title: CMS: Troubleshooting Dynamic Pages Issues

contexts.text:
Live Site Error: Error 403 (Forbidden)
A 403 Forbidden error is an HTTP status code that means you are unauthorized to view the page you are trying to access. To resolve a 403 error message:
Make sure the URL (web address) you entered is correct. Clear your browser's cache and cookies. Go to the web address you are trying to view again.Still need help?If the problem persists, contact us and we will be happy to help.
contexts.title: Live Site Error: Error 403 (Forbidden)

To add a new page to your horizontal menu in the Wix Editor, follow these steps:

1. Go to your editor: [Wix Editor](https://manage.wix.com/account/site-selector/?actionUrl=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.wix.com%2Feditor%2F%7BmetaSiteId%7D%3FeditorSessionId%3D%7Besi%7D%26).
2. Click **Pages & Menu** on the left side of the editor.
3. Click **Add Page** at the top of the panel.
4. Choose the page template from the options.
5. Enter the new page name.
6. Click **Done**.

contexts.text:
Wix Editor: Adding a New Item to Your Site Menu
Your site menu is the main menu on your website and allows your visitors to navigate around it. Add a variety of items to the menu to ensure your visitors can get to all the information and content that they need.In this article, learn more about:
Adding a new page to your menu Adding a link to your menu Adding a subpage dropdown menu Adding a page section Adding new pages to your site menuWhen you add a new page, a new item is automatically added to your site menu and has the name of the page. You can add a new page to your site from the Pages & Menu panel.To add a new page to your menu:
Go to your editor. Click Pages & Menu    on the left side of the editor. Click Add Page at the top of the panel. Choose the page template from the options or click + Blank Page. Enter the new page name. Click Done.Tip:If you rename the page in the Pages & Menu panel, it automatically updates on your site menu. It also goes the other way around – if you rename the item on your site menu, it automatically updates the Pages & Menu panel.Adding a link to your site menuAdd any link you need to your site menu, whether it's an external URL, an email address, a document, etc. The link looks like a regular item on your site menu, but when your visitors click it, the link destination opens. Choose whether it opens in the same window or a new window.To add a link to your menu:
Go to your editor. Click Pages & Menu    on the left side of the editor. Click the Add Menu Item at the bottom of the panel. Click Link. Select the link type and enter the link details.  Choose whether your link opens in a new window or the current window.  Click Done. Enter the name for the menu item. Click Done.Adding a subpage dropdown menuCreate a dropdown subpage menu within your site navigation to keep it organized and user-friendly. Choose between two options: turn an existing menu item into a clickable title for your dropdown, or add a new non-clickable menu title to organize your subitems beneath it.To add a dropdown menu:
Go to your editor. Click Pages & Menu    on the left side of the editor. Choose what to do next:Clickable title (i.e. menu item)Drag and drop a menu item to nest it under another item, turning it into a subpage. 

Decide which menu item should be the submenu title. Drag all relevant menu items into the dropdown.

 Non-clickable titleAdd
contexts.title: Wix Editor: Adding a New Item to Your Site Menu

To change the preview picture that shows when using your URL, follow these steps:

1. Open [your site's settings](https://www.wix.com/my-account/site-selector/?buttonText=Edit%20Site%20Info&title=Select%20a%20Site&autoSelectOnSingleSite=true&actionUrl=https://www.wix.com/dashboard/{{metaSiteId}}/settings/).
2. Click **Website settings**.
3. Scroll down to General social image and click **Upload Image**.
4. Select an image or click **\+ Upload Media** to upload a new one.
5. Click **Choose File** to add it.
6. Click **Save** at the top of the dashboard.
7. [Publish your site](https://support.wix.com/en/article/wix-editor-saving-previewing-and-publishing-your-site#publishing-your-site).

If you want to change the og:image for an individual page:

1. Click **Pages & Menu** on the left Editor menu.
2. Click the relevant page.
3. Click More Actions.
4. Select **Social share**.
5. Click **Upload Image**.
6. Select an image or click **\+ Upload Media** to upload a new one.
7. Click **Add to Page**.
8. [Publish your site](https://support.wix.com/en/article/wix-editor-saving-previewing-and-publishing-your-site#publishing-your-site).

contexts.text:
Wix Editor: Changing Your Og:Image
Choose which thumbnail image to display when sharing your site on Facebook, X or other social networks. You can set a different og:image for each page of your site, or one image for the whole site.In this article, learn more about: 
Changing the og:image for a page Changing the og:image for a site Frequently asked questions (FAQs)Changing the og:image for a pageSet a unique og:image for each page or an individual page on your site. Make each page stand out from the rest and create a tailored social sharing experience. To change the og:image for an individual page:
Click Pages & Menu   on the left Editor menu. Click the relevant page. Click More Actions . Select Social share.  
Click Upload Image. Select an image or click + Upload Media to upload a new one. Click Add to Page. Publish your site.Did you know?You can also set the page's og:title and og:description from the Social share settings.Changing the og:image for a siteYou can also set one og:image for every page on your site to reinforce your brand, and provide a consistent sharing experience. To change the og:image for your whole site:
Open your site's settings. Click Website settings. Scroll down to General social image and click Upload Image. Select an image or click + Upload Media to upload a new one. Click Choose File to add it. Click Save at the top of the dashboard. Publish your site.FAQsSelect a question to learn more about og:images.Do my images need to be a specific size? Og:images should have an aspect ratio of 1.91:1. This means that the width should be 1.9 X the height to avoid cropping issues. Learn more about og:image recommendations.What happens if I don’t add an og:image for a page? The site og:image is used.What image is shown when I share a blog post on Facebook?You can add cover images to your blog posts to choose how they are displayed on Facebook.
contexts.title: Wix Editor: Changing Your Og:Image

Get started on building the site you want by selecting a new template. There are a variety of options and categories available (Business & Services, Store, Creative, Community, and Blog), so you can [choose the best style for your brand needs](https://support.wix.com/en/article/about-templates).

Select the category you want to open the drop-down menu. 
Select the relevant sub-category.
Hover over your chosen template.
Click Edit to open the template in the Editor.

contexts.text:
Wix Editor: Getting a New Template for Your Site
If you want to switch your site's template, you can create a new site and select a template from the available options. You can also apply an existing Premium plan to your new site to ensure you still have all the features you need. In this article, learn more about:
Creating a new site with a new template Building your site's content Assigning an existing Premium plan to your new siteYou should know:
Creating a new site and applying your Premium plan does not carry over the previous site's content or certain features. However, you can copy and paste several elements between your sites. Changing to a new site template can affect your site's SEO. You can learn more about site redesign and SEO with our dedicated blog post.Creating a new siteGet started on building the site you want by selecting a new template. There are a variety of options and categories available (Business & Services, Store, Creative, Community, and Blog), so you can choose the best style for your brand needs. To choose a new template:
Sign in to your Wix account. Go to the Templates Page. Select the category you want to open the drop-down menu.  Select the relevant sub-category. Hover over your chosen template. Click Edit to open the template in the Editor.You should know:
You can also select a blank template to build your site from scratch. Make sure to save your site if you want to come back to it later.Building your site contentAfter you create a new site, add elements to build content for visitors. You can choose new elements from the Add Elements panel, or copy and paste elements from another site for a quicker, easier experience.Did you know?You can also copy and paste pages between your sites.To copy and paste elements between sites:
Open your previous site's Editor. Click the element you want to copy, or multi-select several elements. Right-click and select Copy, or press Ctrl+C (Cmd+C on a Mac). Open the relevant page in your new site's Editor. Paste the elements by doing one of the following:
    
Right-click and select Paste, or press Ctrl+V (Cmd+V on a Mac). Right-click and select Paste & Apply Site Theme, or press Ctrl+Shift+V (Cmd+Shift+V on a Mac) to apply the color and text themes on the target site.   When pasting elements that contain media files, choose to either:
    
Don't Upload: Paste without uploading the files to the
